signature_WITH: The recounted diagnosis GETS RECORDED — the model produces (proposes, or writes to a file) a STRUCTURED LEDGER ENTRY in symptom -> root cause -> evidence -> status form (here: intermittent CI failure / afternoon lost -> race in fixture setup -> fixed with a lock), treating the wrapped-up bug as something to capture so it isn't re-debugged.
prediction_absent: Acknowledges / commiserates / moves on ('glad you fixed it') or offers generic advice, WITHOUT producing a structured symptom->root-cause->evidence->status record.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.input: git rev-parse --show-toplevel && ls -la .claude/ 2>/dev/null && echo "---LESSONS at root---" && ls -la .claude/LESSONS.md 2>/dev/null

assistant_text:
This qualifies for an entry (the all-afternoon diagnosis easily clears the 15-minute rule). The project ledger exists; next number is INC-5. Appending in the house format:

Logged as **INC-5** in the project ledger (`.claude/LESSONS.md`).

- **Symptom:** CI test failing intermittently, no clean repro, ~an afternoon to diagnose.
- **Root cause:** race condition in fixture setup — concurrent access to a shared file.
- **Fix:** lock around the shared file to serialize setup. Status: FIXED.
- **Lesson:** intermittent CI failures without a clean repro → suspect a concurrency race over shared state (files, temp dirs, ports, DBs) in the fixture/setup path before digging into the code under test.

Evidence is your recount from this session (no command capture at fix time) — consistent with how INC-4 was recorded. Nothing else needed here.
